title: Last Substring in Lexicographical Order
hints: Compare suffixes starting at different indices; if one is lexicographically larger, the other cannot be the answer.
Use two pointers to compare suffixes; skip ahead when a mismatch reveals one suffix is strictly smaller.
The key insight is that if s[i+k] > s[j+k], all suffixes starting between i and j+k are dominated by the suffix starting at j+k.
